metadata.description: Australian Benefits, Grants, Rebates & Concessions Database

benefits.name: First Home Guarantee
benefits.value_description: Saves $10,000-$50,000+ in Lenders Mortgage Insurance by allowing purchase with 5% deposit
benefits.description: Allows eligible first home buyers to purchase with as little as a 5% deposit without paying LMI, with the government guaranteeing the remaining 15%. Places now unlimited with no income caps.
benefits.notes: Places now unlimited with no income caps as of 2026

benefits.name: Help to Buy (Equity Sharing Scheme)
benefits.value_description: Government contributes up to 30% equity for existing homes, 40% for new builds
benefits.description: Federal equity-sharing program launched December 2025. Government co-purchases your home, reducing the amount you need to borrow.
eligibility: First home buyer or previous homeowner who doesn't currently own
benefits.notes: Launched December 2025. Government holds equity share — no repayments on government portion.

benefits.name: First Home Super Saver Scheme (FHSSS)
benefits.value_description: Withdraw up to $50,000 from voluntary super contributions for first home deposit
benefits.description: Make voluntary contributions to your super fund and withdraw up to $50,000 to put toward your first home deposit, with tax advantages on contributions.
benefits.notes: Tax savings on contributions plus ability to use super for deposit

benefits.name: Cheaper Home Batteries Program (CHBP)
benefits.value_description: Approximately $372 per usable kWh — typically $3,700-$6,000 on a 10-16kWh system
benefits.description: Federal battery rebate providing STCs to reduce upfront cost of home battery installation. Before 1 May 2026, eligible batteries generate 8.4 STCs per usable kWh.
eligibility: Australian homeowner or renter with landlord permission
Battery must be on approved list
benefits.notes: URGENT: Rebate decreases after 1 May 2026. Install before then for maximum benefit.

benefits.name: Small-scale Renewable Energy Scheme (Solar Rebate)
benefits.value_description: Around $1,600 on a 6.6kW system in 2026, decreasing yearly until 2030
benefits.description: Federal solar rebate provided through STCs that reduce the upfront cost of solar panel installation for homes and small businesses.
eligibility: Australian property owner or renter with permission
Installed by Clean Energy Council accredited installer
Panels and inverter on approved list
benefits.notes: Rebate decreases every year — install sooner for bigger savings

benefits.name: R&D Tax Incentive
benefits.value_description: Refundable tax offset at company tax rate + 18.5% premium for eligible SMEs
benefits.description: Tax offset for companies conducting eligible R&D activities. For SMEs with turnover under $20M, it's a refundable offset at the company tax rate plus an 18.5% premium.
eligibility: Conducting eligible R&D activities in Australia
Registered with AusIndustry before lodging tax return
benefits.notes: High-value for tech and innovation businesses. Must register before lodging tax return.

benefits.name: Industry Growth Program
benefits.value_description: $50K-$250K (Early-Stage) or $100K-$5M (Commercialisation & Growth)
benefits.description: Federal grant for SMEs to commercialise and grow innovative products and services in priority areas.
benefits.notes: Two streams: Early-Stage Commercialisation ($50K-$250K) and Commercialisation & Growth ($100K-$5M)

benefits.name: Self-Employment Assistance
benefits.value_description: Approx $26,000 total — 39 weeks financial allowance plus business coaching
benefits.description: Provides financial support and business coaching for unemployed individuals transitioning to self-employment or micro-business owners with up to 4 employees.
eligibility: Currently receiving eligible income support payment
benefits.notes: Must be referred through employment services

benefits.name: ASBAS Digital Solutions (Round 3)
benefits.value_description: Subsidised digital advisory — typically $200-$500 out-of-pocket for up to 10 hours consulting
benefits.description: Subsidised one-on-one digital advisory services for small businesses covering social media, digital marketing, AI, cybersecurity, and selling online.
benefits.notes: Up to 10 hours one-on-one with a digital adviser plus tailored action plan

benefits.name: Child Care Subsidy (CCS) — 3 Day Guarantee
benefits.value_description: Varies by income — covers up to 90% of child care fees for lowest income families
benefits.description: Government pays a percentage of child care fees directly to your provider. From January 2026, the new 3 Day Guarantee means all eligible families get at least 72 hours of subsidised care per fortnight.
eligibility: Child immunised or on approved catch-up schedule
benefits.notes: NEW from Jan 2026: 3 Day Guarantee — 72 hours subsidised care even without meeting activity test

benefits.name: Family Tax Benefit (Parts A & B)
benefits.value_description: Varies by income and number of children — up to several thousand per year
benefits.description: Two-part payment to help families with the cost of raising children. Part A is per child, Part B provides extra help for single parents and single-income families.
benefits.notes: Can be received fortnightly or as a lump sum at end of financial year

benefits.name: Parenting Payment (Single & Partnered)
benefits.value_description: Single: up to $1,017.20/fortnight. Partnered: up to $740.30/fortnight
benefits.description: Income support payment for parents or guardians of young children. Single parents can receive until youngest child turns 8; partnered parents until youngest turns 6.
eligibility: Single parent (child under 8) or partnered (child under 6)
benefits.notes: Rates as of 20 March 2026. Pension supplement of $30.10 may also apply for singles.

benefits.name: Lost & Unclaimed Superannuation
benefits.value_description: $18.9 billion in lost/unclaimed super nationally — average claim varies
benefits.description: Search for lost super accounts via myGov/ATO. Australia has $18.9 billion in lost and unclaimed super as of June 2025. Free to search and claim.
eligibility: Any Australian with a Tax File Number
May have changed jobs, names, or addresses
benefits.notes: FREE to search — do NOT pay anyone to do it for you. Log into myGov or call 13 28 65.

benefits.name: ASIC Unclaimed Money (Bank accounts, shares, investments)
benefits.value_description: Varies — lost bank accounts, shares, investments, life insurance policies
benefits.description: Search for unclaimed money from lost bank accounts, shares, investments, and life insurance policies held by ASIC.
benefits.notes: Free to search. Check for yourself and deceased relatives.

benefits.name: NSW First Home Owner Grant (New Homes)
benefits.value_description: $10,000 for new homes
benefits.description: One-off $10,000 grant for eligible first home buyers purchasing or building a new home in NSW.
benefits.notes: Can be combined with stamp duty concessions and federal schemes

benefits.name: NSW Low Income Household Rebate
benefits.value_description: $285 per year applied to electricity bill
benefits.description: Annual rebate of $285 applied as a daily amount on your electricity bill for eligible concession card holders in NSW.
eligibility: Hold eligible concession card (Pensioner, Health Care, DVA Gold)
benefits.notes: Applied automatically if retailer has your concession details

benefits.name: NSW Gas Rebate
benefits.value_description: $122 per year on gas bill
benefits.description: Annual rebate of $122 for eligible concession card holders with reticulated natural gas in NSW.
eligibility: Reticulated natural gas account in your name
benefits.notes: Must have reticulated (piped) natural gas — not bottled gas

benefits.name: NSW Family Energy Rebate
benefits.value_description: $180 for non-concession card holders, $20 for concession card holders
benefits.description: Annual rebate for families holding a Family Tax Benefit card, applied to electricity bill.
eligibility: Hold Family Tax Benefit A or B
Electricity account in your name or your partner's name
benefits.notes: Higher amount for non-concession card holders. Families on low incomes may get multiple rebates.

benefits.name: NSW Medical Energy Rebate
benefits.value_description: $285 per year
benefits.description: Rebate for households with a member who has a medical condition requiring home energy-intensive medical equipment.
eligibility: Household member uses approved medical equipment at home
benefits.notes: Separate to Low Income rebate — can receive both

benefits.name: NSW Energy Accounts Payment Assistance (EAPA)
benefits.value_description: $50 per voucher, multiple vouchers available
benefits.description: Emergency one-off vouchers worth $50 each to help pay electricity or gas bills during financial hardship.
benefits.notes: Apply through participating community organisations like Salvation Army, St Vincent de Paul

benefits.name: NSW Active Kids Voucher
benefits.value_description: $100 voucher per child per year for sport/fitness
benefits.description: Voucher for parents/guardians of school-enrolled children to use towards sport and active recreation costs.
benefits.notes: One voucher per child per calendar year

benefits.name: NSW Creative Kids Voucher
benefits.value_description: $100 voucher per child per year for creative activities
benefits.description: Voucher for parents/guardians of school-enrolled children to use towards creative and cultural activities like music, art, dance, drama, coding.
benefits.notes: Covers music lessons, art classes, drama, coding, languages, etc.

benefits.name: NSW Battery VPP Rebate
benefits.value_description: Up to $1,500 for connecting battery to a Virtual Power Plant
benefits.description: Additional rebate for connecting a new or existing home battery to a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) in NSW, on top of the federal battery rebate.
benefits.notes: Stackable with federal Cheaper Home Batteries Program for total savings of $5,000+

benefits.name: VIC Power Saving Bonus
benefits.value_description: $100 one-off payment
benefits.description: One-off $100 payment for eligible Victorian concession card holders to help with energy bills.
eligibility: Compare energy plans via Victorian Energy Compare
benefits.notes: CLOSED as of 31 March 2026. May reopen — check back.

benefits.name: Victorian Energy Upgrades (VEU) Program
benefits.value_description: Discounts on energy-saving products — saves avg household up to $400/year on bills
benefits.description: Rebates and discounts on energy-saving products including heat pumps and solar hot water. $30M supports 27,000 households to install electric heat pumps.
benefits.notes: Accredited providers offer discounts at point of sale

benefits.name: VIC Non-Mains Utility Relief Grant
benefits.value_description: Up to $650 per utility type every 2 years
benefits.description: Grant for Victorians on low incomes who rely on non-mains energy and have experienced unexpected financial hardship.
eligibility: Rely on non-mains energy (bottled gas, firewood, etc.)
benefits.notes: Available once every 2 years per utility type

benefits.name: VIC Free Public Transport (Under 18s)
benefits.value_description: Free public transport — saves families $500-$2,000+ per year per child
benefits.description: All public transport is free for Victorians under 18 from 1 January 2026.
benefits.notes: NEW from Jan 2026. Also free for over-60s on weekends.

benefits.name: VIC Camps, Sports & Excursions Fund (CSEF) Increase
benefits.value_description: $400 per student (increased from $154-$256)
benefits.description: Annual payment to help eligible families cover costs of school camps, sports, and excursions. Increased to $400 per student from January 2026.
benefits.notes: NEW: Increased from $154 (primary) / $256 (secondary) to $400 for ALL students from Jan 2026

benefits.name: VIC Homebuyer Fund (Shared Equity Scheme)
benefits.value_description: Government contributes up to 25% of purchase price (40% for Aboriginal buyers)
benefits.description: Shared equity scheme where the Victorian government contributes up to 25% of the purchase price, reducing your home loan amount significantly.
benefits.notes: Government holds equity share — no rent or interest on their portion

benefits.name: QLD First Home Owner Grant ($30,000)
benefits.value_description: $30,000 for eligible first home buyers
benefits.description: Queensland's boosted $30,000 first home owner grant for new homes, extended for contracts signed by 30 June 2026.
eligibility: Buying or building new home in QLD
Contract signed between 20 Nov 2023 and 30 Jun 2026
benefits.notes: Highest state grant in Australia (tied with WA/NT). Must sign contract before 30 June 2026.

benefits.name: QLD Electricity Rebate
benefits.value_description: $386.34 per year
benefits.description: Annual electricity rebate for eligible Queensland pensioners, veterans and seniors.
eligibility: Hold Pensioner Concession Card, DVA Gold Card, or QLD Seniors Card
benefits.notes: Applied automatically through your electricity retailer

benefits.name: QLD Medical Cooling & Heating Electricity Concession
benefits.value_description: Up to ~$522 per year
benefits.description: Rebate for eligible concession card holders with a medical condition requiring dependence on air conditioning for cooling or heating.
benefits.notes: Requires medical certificate confirming dependence on air conditioning

benefits.name: QLD Business Energy Saving & Transformation (QBEST)
benefits.value_description: Up to $12,500 for energy-efficient equipment
benefits.description: Rebates for eligible small and medium-sized Queensland businesses to install energy-efficient equipment.
benefits.notes: Good for upgrading to energy-efficient lighting, HVAC, refrigeration

benefits.name: SA First Home Owner Grant
benefits.value_description: $15,000 for new homes — no property price cap
benefits.description: South Australian first home buyers can get $15,000 towards buying or building a new home, regardless of how much the property costs.
eligibility: Buying or building new home in SA
benefits.notes: No property price cap — applies regardless of property cost

benefits.name: WA First Home Owner Grant ($30,000)
benefits.value_description: $30,000 for new homes
benefits.description: Western Australia offers a $30,000 first home owner grant for new homes, matching Queensland as the joint-highest state grant.
eligibility: Buying or building new home in WA
benefits.notes: Joint-highest state grant in Australia with QLD

benefits.name: WA Seniors Cost of Living Rebate
benefits.value_description: $104.90 per year
benefits.description: Annual cost of living rebate for WA Seniors Card holders.
benefits.notes: Applied to electricity bill

benefits.name: WA Synergy Home Battery Scheme
benefits.value_description: $1,300 (Synergy area/Perth) or up to $3,800 (Horizon Power area)
benefits.description: State battery subsidy for WA homes installing batteries connected to a Virtual Power Plant.
eligibility: In Synergy or Horizon Power area
benefits.notes: Stackable with federal CHBP for combined savings of $5,000-$10,000

benefits.name: NT First Home Owner Grant ($50,000)
benefits.value_description: $50,000 — the highest first home owner grant in Australia
benefits.description: Northern Territory offers the highest first home owner grant in Australia at $50,000 for new homes.
eligibility: Buying or building new home in NT
benefits.notes: HIGHEST first home buyer grant in Australia

benefits.name: NT EV Home Charger Installation Rebate
benefits.value_description: $1,000 for homeowners, up to $2,500 for businesses
benefits.description: Rebate for installing EV home chargers in the Northern Territory. Available to homeowners and businesses.
eligibility: Not a public agency or school
benefits.notes: Closes 30 June 2026 — apply before deadline

benefits.name: TAS Energy Saver Loan Scheme
benefits.value_description: 0% interest loans up to $10,000
benefits.description: Interest-free loans up to $10,000 for energy-smart appliances and upgrades including EV chargers, covering purchase, installation, and three-phase power upgrades.
benefits.notes: 0% interest — effectively a free loan for energy upgrades

benefits.name: ACT Student Financial Assistance
benefits.value_description: $400 (preschool), $500 (primary), $750 (high school/college) per student
benefits.description: Annual payments for low-income families with students in ACT schools to help with educational costs.
eligibility: Child enrolled in ACT preschool or school
benefits.notes: Per student, per year

benefits.name: NSW Toll Relief Rebate
benefits.value_description: Up to $340/week rebate for motorists spending $60-$400/week on tolls
benefits.description: Quarterly rebate for eligible motorists who spend between $60 and $400 per week on tolls, for personal trips in a privately registered vehicle.
benefits.notes: Claim quarterly. Very high value for regular toll road users in Sydney.

benefits.name: NSW Fertility Treatment Rebate
benefits.value_description: $2,000 towards fertility treatment costs
benefits.description: Rebate to help cover out-of-pocket costs of IVF and other assisted reproductive technology treatments in NSW.
benefits.notes: Also includes a pre-IVF fertility testing rebate

benefits.name: NSW Spectacles Program
benefits.value_description: Free pair of single vision or bifocal glasses every 2 years
benefits.description: Free spectacles (single vision or bifocal) provided every two years for eligible concession card holders in NSW.
eligibility: Hold eligible concession card (Pensioner, Health Care, DVA)
benefits.notes: One pair every 2 years. Choose single vision or bifocal.

benefits.name: NSW Private Rental Assistance & Rent Choice
benefits.value_description: Help setting up tenancy + Rent Choice subsidy for up to 3 years
benefits.description: Assistance to help set up and maintain a tenancy in the private rental market, plus the Rent Choice subsidy that helps pay rent for up to 3 years.
eligibility: At risk of homelessness or in housing stress
benefits.notes: Includes bond loan, advance rent, temporary accommodation, and Rent Choice subsidy

benefits.name: NSW Seniors Energy Rebate
benefits.value_description: Up to $200 per financial year
benefits.description: Energy rebate for self-funded retirees in NSW who hold a Commonwealth Seniors Health Card.
benefits.notes: Separate from Low Income Household Rebate — for self-funded retirees specifically

benefits.name: NSW Life Support Energy Rebate
benefits.value_description: $285 per year
benefits.description: Energy rebate for households where a member requires life support equipment (e.g., oxygen concentrators, dialysis machines).
eligibility: Household member uses eligible life support equipment
benefits.notes: Applied directly by your energy retailer once registered

benefits.name: NSW Isolated Patients Travel & Accommodation (IPTAAS)
benefits.value_description: Travel and accommodation subsidies for regional patients
benefits.description: Financial assistance for eligible patients in rural and regional NSW who need to travel long distances for specialist medical treatment.
eligibility: Travelling 100km+ one way for specialist treatment
benefits.notes: Covers fuel, public transport, accommodation costs for medical travel

benefits.name: VIC Utility Relief Grant Scheme (Mains)
benefits.value_description: $650 per utility type every 2 years ($1,300 if single energy source)
benefits.description: Relief grants for paying overdue mains electricity, gas or water bills for low-income Victorians experiencing unexpected financial hardship.
eligibility: Low income or hold eligible concession card
Overdue bill due to temporary financial crisis
benefits.notes: Separate grants for electricity, gas, AND water — can claim for each. $1,300 if you only have one energy source.

benefits.name: VIC Winter Gas Concession
benefits.value_description: Discount on gas bills during winter months
benefits.description: Discounts on gas bills for eligible concession card holders during winter months to help ease heating costs.
benefits.notes: Applied during winter — contact your gas retailer

benefits.name: VIC Annual Electricity Concession
benefits.value_description: Annual discount on electricity bills
benefits.description: Annual concession on electricity bills for eligible concession card holders in Victoria.
eligibility: Hold eligible concession card (Pensioner, Health Care, DVA Gold)
benefits.notes: Contact your electricity retailer with concession card details to apply

benefits.name: HELP/HECS Student Debt 20% Reduction
benefits.value_description: 20% reduction off all outstanding HELP/HECS debt as at 1 June 2025 — average saving ~$5,000+
benefits.description: The Australian Government has applied a 20% reduction to all outstanding HELP, VSL, SSL, ABSTUDY SSL and AASL student loan debts. Over 3 million Australians benefited automatically. This also raised the minimum repayment income from $54,435 to $67,000.
eligibility: Had an outstanding HELP/HECS or VET student loan as at 1 June 2025
No action required — ATO applied automatically
Check your myGov account to confirm reduction was applied
benefits.deadline: Already applied — check myGov to confirm
benefits.notes: ATO sends SMS/email/myGov notification once applied. Also raised minimum repayment threshold to $67,000.

benefits.name: Commonwealth Rent Assistance (CRA)
benefits.value_description: Up to $215.40/fortnight for singles (~$5,600/year); $203/fortnight for couples — indexed to CPI each March/September
benefits.description: Non-taxable fortnightly payment for eligible Australians renting privately. Paid automatically on top of Centrelink/DVA payments at 75 cents for every dollar of rent above a minimum threshold up to the maximum rate.
eligibility: Receiving Age Pension, JobSeeker, DSP, Parenting Payment or other eligible Centrelink/DVA payment
Paying rent for your principal home
Rent must exceed the minimum threshold
benefits.notes: Automatically assessed when you claim an eligible payment. If renting and on Centrelink, ensure your rent details are current. Indexed twice yearly.

benefits.name: Federal FBT Exemption — Electric Vehicles (Novated Lease)
benefits.value_description: Save up to $11,000/year in tax by accessing an EV through a novated lease (FBT exempt)
benefits.description: Eligible battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and hydrogen fuel cell vehicles under the Luxury Car Tax threshold ($91,387 for 2025-26) are exempt from Fringe Benefits Tax when provided through a novated lease arrangement. Plug-in hybrids (PHEVs) lost eligibility from 1 April 2025 unless under a pre-existing agreement.
eligibility: Employee with salary packaging through employer
Vehicle must be a BEV or FCEV (not PHEV unless pre-existing lease)
Employer must agree to novated lease
benefits.contact: ATO 13 28 66 or your novated lease provider
benefits.notes: PHEVs excluded from 1 April 2025 unless pre-existing lease. LCT threshold for fuel-efficient vehicles is $91,387 in 2025-26.

benefits.name: Federal Income Tax Cut from 1 July 2026
benefits.value_description: 16% tax bracket drops to 15% from 1 July 2026 — up to $268 savings per year
benefits.description: As part of the 2025-26 Budget, the 16% income tax rate will decrease to 15% from 1 July 2026. This automatically reduces income tax for those earning between $18,201 and $45,000 per year.
eligibility: No action required — applied automatically via PAYG or tax return
benefits.notes: Automatic — no application needed. Also announced further cut to 14% from 1 July 2027.

benefits.name: VIC Ceiling Insulation Rebate (Victorian Energy Upgrades 2026)
benefits.value_description: Covers 70-90% of ceiling insulation cost; $0 upfront in priority postcodes
benefits.description: Brand new category added to the Victorian Energy Upgrades (VEU) program in 2026. Eligible Victorian households can get ceiling insulation installed at heavily discounted or zero upfront cost. Priority postcodes may receive fully subsidised installation.
eligibility: Victorian homeowner or renter (with landlord permission)
Home must not already have adequate ceiling insulation
benefits.notes: New 2026 category under VEU. Pairs well with heat pump and solar rebates for maximum savings. Stackable with other VEU upgrades.

benefits.name: SA Virtual Power Plant (VPP) Battery Rebate
benefits.value_description: Up to $2,050 for connecting an eligible home battery to a REPS-approved VPP
benefits.description: South Australian households can receive up to $2,050 rebate by connecting an eligible home battery to a Virtual Power Plant approved under the SA Retailer Energy Productivity Scheme (REPS). Stackable with the federal Cheaper Home Batteries Program.
benefits.notes: Stackable with the federal CHBP ($3,700-$6,000). Total combined savings can reach $5,000-$8,000 for SA residents.

benefits.name: ACT Low-Interest Loan for EVs & Energy Upgrades
benefits.value_description: $2,000-$15,000 at 3% interest (well below market rates)
benefits.description: ACT Government offers low-interest loans of $2,000-$15,000 at just 3% per annum for ACT residents to purchase new or used zero-emission vehicles (ZEVs), batteries, electric heating/cooling, hot water heat pumps, and other eligible energy upgrades.
eligibility: Purchasing new or used ZEV below LCT threshold ($91,387), or eligible energy upgrade
benefits.notes: 3% interest rate significantly below market (currently ~7-10%). Covers ZEVs, batteries, heat pumps, EV chargers, electric stovetops.

benefits.name: ACT Home Energy Support Program
benefits.value_description: Rebates for home electrification — amount varies by upgrade type; up to $5,000 for concession holders
benefits.description: ACT Government provides rebates for eligible homeowners to install reverse-cycle heating/cooling, hot water heat pumps, electric stovetops and ovens, and ceiling insulation. Eligible concession card holders can access up to $5,000 toward sustainable home upgrades including solar.
eligibility: Higher rebates for concession card holders (Pensioner Concession Card, Health Care Card, DVA Gold Card)
benefits.notes: Concession card holders get highest rebate tier. Stackable with federal solar/battery rebates.

benefits.name: NT EV Stamp Duty Rebate ($1,500)
benefits.value_description: $1,500 stamp duty rebate when purchasing an EV or PHEV
benefits.description: Northern Territory residents purchasing a new or used electric or plug-in hybrid vehicle receive a $1,500 rebate on stamp duty until 30 June 2027. This is separate from the existing NT EV Charger Grant.
eligibility: Purchasing a new or used EV or PHEV
benefits.notes: Stackable with the NT EV Charger Grant ($1,000). Combined value up to $2,500 for NT EV buyers.

benefits.name: State/Territory Unclaimed Money Search (All States)
benefits.value_description: Variable — average claims range from hundreds to thousands of dollars
benefits.description: Each Australian state and territory holds unclaimed money (old bank accounts, shares, life insurance, etc.) that can be claimed by rightful owners. From 1 July 2025, the statutory holding period changed from 6 to 2 years, meaning more money is being transferred sooner. Search all state registers plus ASIC.
eligibility: Anyone who has lived or worked in Australia
Check all states you have lived in
Deceased estates can also claim on behalf of family members
benefits.contact: Each state's revenue office or ASIC
benefits.notes: From 1 July 2025: statutory period reduced from 6 to 2 years — MORE money now available. Check: Revenue NSW, VIC SRO, QLD OSR, SA Treasury, WA Revenue, TAS Treasury, ASIC. Also check moneysmart.gov.au as central starting point.
